title: "tbox-finder — Phase 1 (Backbones & heads) dev-log"
author: "bioedca"
format:
  typst:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

<!-- Rendered with the Typst engine (quarto ≥1.9, `format: typst`): the
     laptop/CI toolchain ships no LaTeX, and Typst produces the PDF natively.
     Renders via `conda run -n tbox-viz quarto render analyses/phase1_log.qmd`. -->


# Phase 1 — Backbones & heads

Per-step development log (CLAUDE.md §2.4). One stanza is appended after every
step's commit; this file renders to `docs/dev-log/phase1_<YYYY-MM-DD>.pdf` at
the phase-exit gate.

## Step P1-01 — 2026-07-12: Stage-1 CUDA-kernel import/forward smoke gate + pure-PyTorch fallback cost

**Inputs.** The pinned GPU env **`tbox-ml-dna`** (`envs/ml-dna.yml` + `ml-dna.conda-lock.yml`:
torch 2.7.1+cu128, mamba-ssm 2.2.6.post3, causal-conv1d 1.6.2.post1, flash-attn 2.8.3.post1);
the P0-06 `sm_86` wheel-availability finding (ADR-0002 A2); a seeded random-DNA token fixture
(`tests/fixtures/kernel_smoke_dna/dna_tokens.npy`, seed 42, `[2,512]` int8).

**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/kernels/kernel_smoke.py` (run via `slurm/p1/kernel_smoke.sbatch` on
one cluster RTX A4000, Ampere `sm_86`, driver 590.48.01) probes the runtime, asserts
`import selective_scan_cuda` (ADR-0002 A2 C2), embeds the DNA fixture into selective-scan inputs
(fp32; D=512, N=16, L=512, B=2), and forwards through both the CUDA kernels
(`selective_scan_fn`/`causal_conv1d_fn`) and the hand-wired pure-PyTorch references
(`selective_scan_ref`/`causal_conv1d_ref`), recording kernel-vs-ref max-abs parity and tokens/sec.
Kernel APIs were verified against the library source at the pinned tags (v2.2.6.post3 / v1.6.2.post1)
**and** the installed env (context7 quota-blocked). The first run (job 460) exposed a partial
`causal-conv1d` install in the cluster env (orphan `.so`, no package/dist-info) — repaired by
reinstalling the exact pinned wheel; the smoke was hardened to record import failures in-report
rather than crash (the job-460 crash left no report).

**Outputs.** `reports/p1/kernel_smoke.json` (git_sha `8ba3880`, SLURM job 462, `measured=true`);
`src/tbox_finder/kernels/{__init__,kernel_smoke}.py`; `slurm/p1/kernel_smoke.sbatch`;
`tests/unit/test_kernel_smoke_report.py` (14 cases, CPU-only); `tests/fixtures/kernel_smoke_{dna,sample}/`;
**ADR-0002 Amendment A5**.

**Validation.** **Gate PASSED** (`gate.overall_pass=true`): import + forward green on an A4000 `sm_86`
(`selective_scan_cuda` + `causal_conv1d_cuda` + mamba-ssm/causal-conv1d/flash-attn all import);
`selective_scan` parity **1.53e-5** and `causal_conv1d` **9.5e-7** (both ≪ `atol` 1e-2); measured
pure-PyTorch `selective_scan` fallback throughput ratio **≈871×** (CUDA 12.46 M vs ref 14.3 K tok/s);
`causal_conv1d` **0.56×** (the D3 optional accelerator — small-tensor launch overhead, correctness is
what the gate needs). Artifact-based verification (`sacct` off): `squeue` empty + `kernel_smoke.DONE`
present + zero-byte `.err`.

**Decisions.** The "pure-PyTorch fallback" is `selective_scan_ref` invoked directly — the packaged
`Mamba` block never reaches it (ADR-0002 A2 C2), so the ≈871× slowdown quantitatively confirms the
prebuilt `sm_86` wheel is **load-bearing**, not a convenience. `atol` is an implementer choice (ADR-0002
pins no kernel-vs-fallback tolerance; the raw diff is always recorded, §10.3). Cross-link **ADR-0002 A5**.

**Commit.** `8ba3880` (scaffold `5f18180` + hardening `8ba3880`) + the follow-up carrying this stanza,
the report, and A5; squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in `TODO.md` at P1-01 close.

## Step P1-02 — 2026-07-12: Load Caduceus-PS default checkpoint + human-reference pretraining provenance

**Inputs.** The default Stage-1 checkpoint — Caduceus-PS
`kuleshov-group/caduceus-ps_seqlen-131k_d_model-256_n_layer-16` (7.73 M params, `d_model` 256,
16 MambaDNA layers, RC-equivariant) — pinned at the **immutable commit** `d89eeb85…` (never `main`);
the `tbox-ml-dna` env (transformers 4.57.5 `trust_remote_code` ceiling; ADR-0002 D2/A4); a short
seeded DNA fixture + its reverse-complement built in token space via the config `complement_map`.

**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/models/caduceus_backbone.py::load_caduceus_ps` loads the backbone
(`AutoModel`, `trust_remote_code=True`) at the pinned revision on the **local GPU** (RTX 4060, `sm_89`),
counts parameters, and measures RC-equivariance — `f(RC(x))` vs `f(x).flip(dims=(-2,-1))` (the
model-card hidden-state RC transform) — with a reverse-only negative control. The CLI writes the
canonical `data/interim/caduceus_ps_131k/provenance.json` sidecar (`provenance.py`; ADR-0002 as the
pinning ADR). **imp.md↔ADR reconciliation:** imp.md's "pure-PyTorch CPU forward" is superseded by
ADR-0002 A2 C2 (the packaged `Mamba` hard-imports `selective_scan_cuda`; no CPU forward exists) —
resolved via the local GPU (still LOCAL, no SLURM). Loader/RC APIs verified against the installed
transformers 4.57.5 + the HF model card (context7 quota-blocked).

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/models/{__init__,caduceus_backbone}.py`; `conf/model/caduceus_stage1.yaml`;
`data/interim/caduceus_ps_131k/provenance.json` (git-tracked, `measured=true`, git_sha `bd64aa6`);
`tests/unit/test_caduceus_backbone.py` (23 cases: 21 bare + 2 GPU). **No DVC artifact** (metadata
sidecar; weights are pulled from HF by revision, not stored).

**Validation.** **Gate PASSED** (`gate.overall_pass=true`): loads at the pinned revision; **param count
== 7,725,312** (== `EXPECTED_PARAM_COUNT`, the PRD "7.73 M" to the exact integer); **RC-equivariance
max-abs-diff 0.0** ≤ `atol` 1e-4 (bit-exact), reverse-only negative control **2.39** (the check
discriminates); provenance carries `pretraining.domain = "human reference genome"` + ≥2 citations + the
`ml-dna` env-lock hash. Bare tier **21 passed / 2 skipped** (the CI path); GPU tier green under
`tbox-ml-dna` (§8.5 manual gate); `TBOX_REQUIRE_CADUCEUS=1` rot-guard fails closed; full unit suite
**396 passed / 53 skipped** (no regression); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean.

**Decisions.** The checkpoint is pinned by an **immutable commit SHA** (never `main`, ADR-0002 D2); the
param count + equivariance are **frozen in code** (no config can weaken them — the coverage.py
`OOD_ECE_MIN_N` precedent). RC-equivariance is asserted on the **hidden states** (what the P1-03 head
consumes) via the model-card `flip(dims=(-2,-1))` transform — the §6 strand-resolver precondition.
**Scientific-evidence gate (§10.1) CLEARED** for the load-bearing human-reference pretraining fact:
≥2 authoritative agreeing sources — HF model card (verbatim) + Schiff et al. (arXiv:2403.03234;
PMID:40567809), both already in-repo (ADR-0002). The gate is **hardware-agnostic** (`sm_89` laptop;
RC-equivariance is an architectural property of RCPS, identical on the cluster A4000 `sm_86`).
Cross-link **ADR-0002 D7**.

**Commit.** feature-branch atomic commit on `p1-02-caduceus` (squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in
`TODO.md` at P1-02 close); feeds **P1-03** (seg-head build), **P1-04** (frozen-embedding probe),
**P1-09** (continued-pretraining).

## Step P1-03 — 2026-07-12: Stage-1 per-position 8-class segmentation head (Linear + optional CRF)

**Inputs.** The 8 normative class identifiers (ADR-0004 D1), single-sourced from
`tbox_finder.labels.CLASS_ORDER`; a random `L×d_model` hidden-state fixture (`torch.randn`, no backbone
load needed). No in-repo segmentation head existed — it had to be built (PRD §6/§8/§10.1).

**Process.** Built `SegmentationHead` (`src/tbox_finder/models/seg_head.py::SegmentationHead`): a
per-position `Linear(input_dim → 8)` projection over the Caduceus-PS hidden states, with an **optional**
in-repo `LinearChainCRF` transition layer for boundary coherence (`use_crf` knob so P2 can ablate
Linear-only vs Linear+CRF, ADR-0004). The CRF is a standard **batch-first linear-chain CRF** —
`torch.logsumexp` forward algorithm for the partition function, Viterbi for decoding — implemented
in-repo with **no external `torchcrf`/`pytorch-crf` dependency** (the P1-03 note's default, so no env
amendment). The head is **8-class by construction** (`NUM_CLASSES = len(CLASS_ORDER)`); the input width
default `CADUCEUS_PS_HIDDEN_DIM = 2*d_model = 512` is single-sourced from `caduceus_backbone.D_MODEL`
(the RC-concatenated hidden width) so the two cannot drift. `decode()` returns per-sequence best paths
(CRF Viterbi, else per-position argmax); `loss()` is CRF NLL, else masked cross-entropy
(`F.cross_entropy` K-dim form + `ignore_index`).

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/models/seg_head.py` (head + CRF; torch imported at module top — the
submodule *is* a torch model, but the `models` package `__init__` stays lazy so the package still
imports bare); `tests/unit/test_seg_head.py` (24 cases: 3 bare-tier — the ADR-0004 class-order/index +
`2*d_model` contract, run in CI — + a 21-test torch tier under `tbox-ml-dna`). **No DVC artifact**
(library + tests; git-tracked). **No `.smk`/data change** (DAG lint unaffected).

**Validation.** **Gate PASSED** — logits shape `(B,L,8)` (Linear-only and CRF); the CRF `viterbi_decode`
returns a length-L valid class sequence (every index in `[0,8)`); class-index↔identifier mapping matches
the ADR-0004 literal. Torch tier **21 passed** under `tbox-ml-dna` (CPU — no GPU needed); bare CI tier
**3 passed / 18 skipped** (torch tier skips cleanly, no collection error); full unit suite **465 passed /
25 skipped** bare (no regression); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean. Behavioral gates that *bite*: the
CRF reduces to per-position argmax when its transitions vanish, and a stay-in-tag-0 transition bias
overrides the emission argmax (the transition layer is proven load-bearing); partition ≥ gold-path score
⇒ NLL ≥ 0 per sequence; masked decode truncates to the mask lengths.

**Decisions.** In-repo CRF chosen over a third-party dep to keep the step self-contained (no
`envs/ml.yml` amendment / §3.1 approval). Torch lives at the seg_head module top (idiomatic `nn.Module`),
so the bare-CI test tier guards with `skipif(not torch)` and the load-bearing class-order invariant is
asserted torch-free from `tbox_finder.labels` — the head never redeclares the vocabulary, so a logit
index always maps to the ADR-0004 identifier at that position. The RC-combination that feeds this head
stays directionality-preserving upstream (P1-02/P1-04, §6 strand-resolver); this head is
combination-agnostic (it consumes an `(B,L,2·d_model)` hidden state). context7 §3.3: `/pytorch/pytorch`
consulted for `F.cross_entropy` K-dim `(N,C,d)` + `ignore_index` and `nn.Parameter` registration; the
CRF forward/Viterbi correctness is verified by the executed test under the installed torch 2.7.1
(P1-01/P1-02 installed-library-cross-check precedent). Cross-link **ADR-0004 D1**.

**Commit.** feature-branch atomic commit on `p1-03-seg-head` (squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in
`TODO.md` at P1-03 close); feeds **P1-04** (backbone+head assembly with the directionality-preserving
RC-combination knob) and **P1-09** (continued-pretraining consumes the same head).

## Step P1-04 — 2026-07-12: Assemble the Stage-1 segmenter (backbone → RC-combine → seg head) with a directionality-preserving RC-combination knob

**Inputs.** The P1-02 Caduceus-PS backbone (`D_MODEL=256`, `_hidden_states → (B,L,2·d_model)=512`); the
P1-03 `SegmentationHead` (`input_dim=CADUCEUS_PS_HIDDEN_DIM=512`); the 8-class `tbox_finder.labels.CLASS_ORDER`
(Specifier=2, Antiterminator_Tbox_seq=5); a synthetic asymmetric hidden-state fixture + its forward↔RC
channel-half swap (no backbone forward — CPU-testable).

**Process.** Built `src/tbox_finder/models/stage1_segmenter.py::Stage1Segmenter` (an `nn.Module` wiring
backbone → `RCCombine` → `SegmentationHead`) and `RCCombine` — the `rc_combine` knob restricted to
**directionality-preserving (non-averaged)** forms (PRD §6/§10.1; ADR-0002): `concat` (**default**;
identity — feed the full `(fwd‖rc)` 512-d state to the head, injective ⇒ order-preserving) and `gate` (a
learned directional per-channel gate `g⊙fwd+(1-g)⊙rc → d_model`, `g=sigmoid(logit)` init nonzero so
`g≠0.5` ⇒ asymmetric by construction). An order-destroying **mean** (`0.5·(fwd+rc)`) is **rejected** at
construction and is **not the default**. The mode contract (`ALLOWED_RC_COMBINE={concat,gate}`,
`normalize_rc_combine`, `is_directionality_preserving`) is single-sourced from a **torch-free**
`src/tbox_finder/models/rc_combine.py` so bare CI locks it; the segmenter re-exports it. Added the
`rc_combine` group to `conf/model/caduceus_stage1.yaml` (declarative `mode: concat`; the allowed set +
mean-rejection are frozen in code, not config). The backbone forward (`forward(input_ids=…)`, needs CUDA,
ADR-0002 A2 C2) is deferred to the P1-07 fine-tune smoke; the CPU surface is `logits_from_hidden` /
`decode_from_hidden` / `loss_from_hidden`.

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/models/{stage1_segmenter,rc_combine}.py` + `conf/model/caduceus_stage1.yaml`
(+`rc_combine` group) + `tests/unit/test_rc_combination.py` (5 bare-tier + 20 torch-tier). No DVC artifact
(library + tests + config; git-tracked). No ADR amendment (implements the ADR-0002 / PRD §6/§10.1 pinned
constraint; no new decision/threshold).

**Validation.** Unit green. Torch tier **25 passed** under `tbox-ml-dna` (CPU); bare CI tier **5 passed /
20 skipped** (torch tier skips cleanly — the mode contract is locked torch-free); full bare unit suite **no
regression** (0 failures; the new file contributes exactly +5 passed / +20 skipped vs `main`); ruff 0.15.15
+ black 25.11.0 clean; `snakemake --lint` good + `-n` resolves (no `.smk` change, DAG unaffected). The
directionality gate **bites**: the allowed `concat`/`gate` combines are **NOT** invariant under the
forward↔RC channel-half swap (element order survives), whereas a reference symmetric **mean IS** invariant
(order collapses); end-to-end, a `concat` segmenter with a controlled head decodes a **strand-dependent**
element order (`[2,2,0,5,5,0]` = Specifier→Antiterminator for the forward channels vs all-background for the
swapped input), while a mean-combined reference decodes **identically** for a window and its opposite strand
— the exact collapse the §6 strand-resolver cannot tolerate.

**Decisions.** The RC-combination is the load-bearing link Stage-1 → the §6 strand-resolver: orientation is
derived from predicted element order, so an order-averaging combine would defeat it — hence `mean` is not a
selectable mode (not merely a non-default), and the allowed non-averaged set is frozen in code so no config
can weaken it. `concat` is the default because Caduceus-PS's `2·d_model` hidden state already carries the
`(fwd‖rc)` channels and feeding them whole is injective (P1-03's head `input_dim=512` is exactly this path);
`gate` is the second non-averaged form for the P2 RC-combination ablation (PRD §11 Sweeps). The
`swap_strand_channels` fwd/RC half-swap is the channel-space action of "read the opposite strand" — the
minimal symmetry under which an order-destroying average is invariant and a directionality-preserving form
is not. context7 §3.3: `/pytorch/pytorch` consulted for `torch.chunk`/`torch.split`/`torch.cat` + the GLU
split-half+`sigmoid` gating idiom + `nn.Parameter`/`torch.sigmoid`; correctness verified by the executed
test under installed torch 2.7.1+cu128 (P1-01/02/03 installed-library-cross-check precedent). Cross-link
**ADR-0002** (RC-combination constraint / D7) and **PRD §6/§10.1**.

**Commit.** feature-branch atomic commit on `p1-04-segmenter` (squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in
`TODO.md` at P1-04 close); feeds **P1-07** (the binding segmentation smoke fine-tune consumes this
segmenter) and **P1-05/P1-08** (the frozen-embedding probe uses the backbone-free `logits_from_hidden` path).

## Step P1-05 — 2026-07-12: Transfer go/no-go part (i) — frozen-embedding linear-separability pre-filter (cheap, non-binding)
**Inputs.** Frozen Caduceus-PS embeddings (7.73M params, pinned revision `d89eeb85…`, human-reference
pretrained) of **8,715 held-out (leave-clade-out) bacterial T-box windows** (positives — `split_assignments.parquet`
`nested_role == heldout` & `source == corpus`; window DNA from `class_I/II.fa`) vs **2,000 GC-matched prokaryotic
background windows** (negatives — `decoys_v0.parquet` pool `gc_background`; §9.1 class 1). **Process.** Two-stage,
no env change: (A) extract mean-pooled 512-d frozen embeddings on the **laptop GPU** (RTX 4060 sm_89, `tbox-ml-dna`,
~9 min at ~21 windows/s batch=1) → npz cache; (B) fit a `StandardScaler`+`LogisticRegression(class_weight=balanced,
C=1)` probe under `tbox-data` on a **homology-safe split** (`GroupShuffleSplit` by `cluster_id` — §8.2: no sequence
cluster spans the boundary), scored on the held-out probe split with a 2,000-draw seeded AUROC bootstrap CI. Entry
`src/tbox_finder/probes/frozen_linear_probe.py::run_prefilter` (`--stage extract|probe|all`). **Outputs.**
`reports/p1/prefilter_separability.json` (git-tracked, `measured=true`): **AUROC 0.9921** (95% CI 0.9887–0.9950),
**balanced-accuracy 0.9597** on a 2,963-pos / 500-neg held-out probe split; inline provenance (git_sha, both env-lock
hashes, seed 42, input SHA-256s). The 22 MB embeddings npz is a gitignored **regenerable** intermediate (not
DVC-tracked; the report hashes it), per the kernel_smoke precedent.
**Validation.** ADR-0002 D7 part-(i) *above-chance* pre-filter → **PASS** (AUROC-CI-lower 0.989 ≫ chance 0.5);
**non-binding** (a low pre-filter can coexist with a passing full fine-tune — the *binding* gate is the P1-07
per-nucleotide segmentation smoke). Leakage: `cluster_spans_boundary=false`, `n_shared_clusters=0`; positives
held-out-fold only. Tests: **31 passed** (stdlib + sklearn tiers — verdict-bites, validator-bites incl. §8.2
fail-closed, committed-report validity, homology-safe partition); the torch-tier extraction test **passed under
`tbox-ml-dna` on the GPU** (skips in CI); **27 pass bare**. ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean.
**Decisions.** (1) Grouped the probe split by **`cluster_id`, not `resolved_order`**: ~41 clusters span >1 order
and carry ~20% of the held-out positives, so an order-grouped split leaks clusters while dropping them is too lossy
— the §8.2 homology unit (sequence cluster) is load-bearing, and order-spanning across *distinct* clusters is not
homology leakage (reported as informational, `order_spans_boundary=true`). (2) **Two-stage across two envs**
(`tbox-ml-dna` torch + `tbox-data` sklearn) because no single committed env carries both → avoids an ADR-0002 D8
env bump/re-lock. (3) The verdict operationalization (PASS iff AUROC bootstrap-CI-lower > 0.5) is an **implementer
operationalization** of the ADR's qualitative *above-chance* criterion, not a new ADR-pinned number (P0-31 precedent);
ADR-0002 pins no numeric pre-filter floor. context7 §3.3: `/websites/scikit-learn_stable` consulted for
`LogisticRegression`/`GroupShuffleSplit`/`StandardScaler` pipeline; the transformers embedding path reuses the
P1-02-verified `caduceus_backbone` loader (pinned-revision, frozen). Cross-link **ADR-0002 D7**, **PRD §10.1 / §18.2**.
**Commit.** feature-branch atomic commit on `p1-05-frozen-linear-probe` (squash-merge SHA on `main` recorded in
`TODO.md` at P1-05 close); feeds **P1-07** (advisory input to the binding go/no-go adjudication) and
**P1-08** (Stage-1 smoke reproducibility).

## Step P1-06 — 2026-07-12: Held-out prokaryotic per-nt 8-class segmentation smoke set (ADR-0004/0005 labels)
**Inputs.** The committed **git-tracked** split table (`data/processed/splits/split_assignments.parquet`, PRD §9.2);
the DVC-tracked corpus (`master_clean_v0.parquet`) + P0 labels (`labels_v0.parquet`); the P0 label-derivation
function (`tbox_finder.labels.derive_label_codes`, ADR-0004 D1). **Process.** `src/tbox_finder/data/seg_smoke.py::build_smoke_set`
(rule `workflow/rules/backbones.smk::p1_seg_smoke_set`, LOCAL, `tbox-data`) selects held-out loci with the **verbatim
P1-05 predicate** `nested_role=="heldout" & source=="corpus"` (no "prokaryotic" predicate — the corpus is all-bacterial,
no domain column exists), reuses the committed per-nt `label_string` (re-derived via `derive_label_codes` on the same
coords as a **fail-loud consistency guard**, §10.3 — no biology re-derived), and writes one row per locus + a padded
per-nt label matrix. A deterministic, `record_id`-sorted, **rarest-class-first coverage-greedy** selection guarantees
all 8 classes + ≥1 class-II locus (so the class-I-only-Terminator invariant is non-vacuous). **Outputs.**
`data/interim/p1_seg_smoke/windows.parquet` (300 loci; DVC) + `labels.npy` (**(300, 547) int16, pad = −100** = the
ADR-0005 seg-head ignore-index, `allow_pickle=False`; DVC) + `windows.provenance.json` (git) +
`data/processed/audits/seg_smoke_report.json` (git; digest `3d6f77f0…`, per-class coverage, `positives_heldout_only=true`,
239 clusters / 26 orders); the golden CI slice `tests/fixtures/p1_seg_smoke/heldout_slice.csv` (24 real held-out loci
+ coords, CI-reproducible without the DVC tables) + `expected.sha256` (`3c495c04…`).
**Validation.** **Golden PASS** — the smoke digest reproduces `expected.sha256` from a **stdlib-only** CSV re-hash
(bare CI) *and* the pandas rebuild; digests are **PYTHONHASHSEED-independent** (verified seeds 0/1/random/7).
**Coverage PASS** — all 8 classes present; **0** ADR-0004 violations (`terminator_on_class_ii=0`,
`specifier_carveout_violations=0`, `antiterm_term_violations=0`). **Leakage PASS (§8.2)** — 100% of loci are
held-out-fold; the full-corpus `tests/ml/test_no_leakage.py` re-run stays green (P1-06 only *selects* from the
unchanged split table — it perturbs no cluster/taxon membership). Tests: `test_seg_smoke_leakage.py` (20: predicate,
deterministic/sorted selection, class coverage, order-independent digest, +6 fail-closed bite tests incl.
non-held-out / class-II-Terminator / length-mismatch / label-drift, numpy pad tier, committed-fixture 100%-held-out
tier) + `test_seg_smoke_set.py` (7: stdlib + pandas golden, label-are-P0-derivation, coverage/conventions). **Full
suite 569 passed / 49 skipped** (no regression); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean; `snakemake --lint` good + rule
`-n` resolves.
**Decisions.** (1) **CSV fixture** (not parquet): pandas reads it without pyarrow, human-diffable, matches the
`ingest_sample` reuse-a-committed-slice precedent; the split table is git-tracked but master/labels are DVC → the
golden must be self-contained (a digest-critical column set is force-`str` on reload so dtype inference can't alter a
hashed token). (2) **Reuse the committed P0 `label_string`, cross-checked against `derive_label_codes`** — the most
literal "no re-derivation" reading, with drift caught loudly. (3) **Padded 2-D int16 matrix, pad = −100** (not a
ragged/object array): deterministic, hashable, pickle-free (the P1-05 pickle-surface lesson), and directly the
seg-head cross-entropy `ignore_index`. (4) **Smoke digest hashes `(record_id, FASTA_sequence, label_string)`**, sorted
by `record_id` — reuses the `ingest.record_hash`/`records_digest` contract (parquet-byte-independent, the labels_golden
precedent); the golden is a regression on the committed **fixture** slice, not the full 300-locus artifact (its
provenance carries the production digest). context7 §3.3: n/a (reuses in-repo `labels`/`ingest`/`provenance`; pandas/
numpy only for IO). Cross-link **ADR-0004 D1**, **ADR-0005**, **PRD §8/§9.2**.
**Commit.** atomic commit on `p1-06-seg-smoke`; feeds **P1-07** (the BINDING per-nt segmentation smoke fine-tune
consumes this set) + **P1-09** (continued-pretraining).

## Step P1-07 — 2026-07-12: Stage-1 per-nt 8-class segmentation smoke fine-tune + BINDING transfer go/no-go (MEASURED: GO — Stage-1 Caduceus transfer confirmed for P2)
**Inputs.** The P1-04 `Stage1Segmenter` (Caduceus-PS backbone → `concat` RC-combine → 8-class seg head); the P1-06
held-out smoke set `data/interim/p1_seg_smoke/{windows.parquet,labels.npy}` (300 leave-clade-out loci; `FASTA_sequence`
+ per-nt `label_string` self-contained; `labels.npy` (300,547) int16 pad −100); the code-pinned backbone loader
(`load_caduceus_ps`, immutable revision `d89eeb85…`); the P1-05 advisory pre-filter (`reports/p1/prefilter_separability.json`,
`verdict=PASS`, non-binding). **Process.** New Hydra entry `src/tbox_finder/train/stage1_smoke.py::main` → `run_smoke`
(rule `slurm/p1/seg_smoke.sbatch`, SLURM `gpu` A4000, `tbox-ml-dna`): seed + TF32/cudnn-off determinism (§8.3) →
load the self-contained windows (re-establish the `record_id`-sorted positional labels↔windows contract, fail-loud on
any length disagreement) → full fine-tune (backbone + head, ADR-0002 D7 fallback 1) with **per-nt focal CE** (γ=2, PRD
§11; masked to the valid-token mean, pad contributes 0) at **batch=1** over variable-length windows → decode per-nt
argmax → flatten non-pad positions → grade **per-nt F1 over the 3 core elements** via `metrics.gate4_core_min_f1`
(min, not mean; ADR-0004 D6) → adjudicate GO/borderline/NO-GO vs the **background-only baseline** (a class-0-only
predictor scores min-core-F1 = 0.0). **Outputs (code, this commit).** `src/tbox_finder/train/{__init__,stage1_smoke}.py`
+ `conf/train/stage1_smoke.yaml` (seed pinned; `@package _global_`; `defaults: [/model: caduceus_stage1, /tracking: wandb,
/optim: adamw_smoke]`) + `conf/optim/adamw_smoke.yaml` + `slurm/p1/seg_smoke.sbatch` + `tests/ml/test_transfer_gonogo.py`
(24). The **measured** `reports/p1/seg_smoke_gonogo.json` + `checkpoints/p1/seg_smoke/` (DVC) are produced by the SLURM
run (post submit-ack), not this commit.
**Validation.** **Harness PASS (bare CI, 20 Tier-1 tests):** `classify_gonogo` bites GO/borderline/NO-GO + fail-closed
on NaN/at-baseline/non-number; the F1-over-3-core harness is **single-sourced from `metrics.gate4_core_min_f1`** and
matches hand-computed values within 1e-9 (the imp.md "harness computes the gate metric within tolerance", extending the
eval-gate coverage to the transfer context); `validate_report` rejects an inconsistent verdict / wrong baseline /
missing `eval_split` / non-binding / wrong core keys / wrong revision. **Torch tier PASS (4, `tbox-ml-dna`,
`TBOX_REQUIRE_TRANSFER_GATE=1`):** focal recovers plain CE at γ=0, ignores pad, downweights easy examples; the
backbone-free head path yields `(B,L,8)`. **Full CI-exact suite 594 passed / 53 skipped** (no regression); ruff
0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean. **Laptop sizing smoke (SIZING ONLY — NOT the verdict; sm_89, 2 epochs):** end-to-end
runs clean (300 windows / 93,764 valid positions; focal 0.76→0.11), wall **2:27** incl. ~40 s backbone load ⇒
**~20–25 min for 20 epochs** on the A4000 (VRAM tiny at batch=1 → no gradient checkpointing needed); it previews
min-core-F1 **≈0.87** (Specifier the min; Stem_I 0.97, Antiterminator 0.98), an unambiguous GO signal far above the
0.0 baseline and the provisional 0.30 threshold. **MEASURED VERDICT: GO** (SLURM job 464, cluster A4000 sm_86, 20
epochs, seed 42, git_sha `1213883`): **min per-nt F1 over the 3 core elements = 0.99990** (Stem_I the min; Specifier =
Antiterminator = 1.000; focal loss → 0.0010) vs the **background-only baseline 0.0** → decisively above the 0.30 go
threshold (non-marginal). Both parts of the D7 two-part go/no-go are positive (part i P1-05 AUROC 0.992) → **Stage-1
Caduceus prokaryotic transfer confirmed for P2**; the fallback ladder is **not triggered**. Verification: artifact-based
(job 464, sacct off) — `squeue` empty + `seg_smoke.DONE` + self-validated report; `.err` benign (wandb-offline notices
only); provenance git_sha `1213883` (no drift), seed 42, inputs/outputs hashed.
**Decisions.** (1) **Focal CE (γ=2), not the head's plain CE** — the dense-background regime (PRD §11) lets plain CE
collapse a tiny smoke to all-background and manufacture a spurious NO-GO; focal is computed in the trainer over the
segmenter logits, leaving the P1-03 head untouched. (2) **Go/no-go = "clearly above the background-only baseline"
(ADR-0002 D7), NOT the 0.80 GATE-4 floor** (that is P2 on the full split); operationalized as directional thresholds
**GO ≥ 0.30 / NO-GO ≤ 0.10 / borderline → §7 stop-and-ask**, **provisional pending the §7 verdict sign-off + ADR-0002
amendment** (D7 pins the exact small-N threshold to be *set with the fixture* — a go/no-go, not a blinded-frozen gate;
the 0.80 floor stays frozen in `power.py`/`metrics.py`, never weakened here). (3) **eval-on-fine-tune-set = a
learnability/expressivity transfer probe** (D7's "can the backbone learn prokaryotic T-box boundaries"), disclosed
honestly in the report (`data.eval_split="fine_tune_set"`, validator-enforced, §10.3) — true generalization is graded
at P2; a held-out-within-smoke variant is flagged as an option at the verdict Stop. (4) **batch=1 over variable-length
windows** — avoids pad/mask, stays deterministic for the P1-08 seeded re-run. (5) **Hydra first wired here** — the
nested `conf/train/` primary uses `@package _global_` + absolute `/group` defaults to reach the search-path-root groups
(verified by a local compose test: keys land at root, groups + CLI overrides resolve). (6) **Two-env test tiering** —
bare Tier-1 in CI, torch Tier-2 under `tbox-ml-dna` (no torch in CI; the P1-03/P1-04 precedent). context7 §3.3:
`/pytorch/pytorch` (focal via `F.cross_entropy` reduction=none + `exp(-ce)` p_t, ignore_index masking) +
`/facebookresearch/hydra` (`@hydra.main` version_base/config_path, nested-config packaging + `/group` defaults);
verified by the executed tests + the compose smoke under torch 2.7.1+cu128 / hydra 1.3.2. Cross-link **ADR-0002 D7**,
**ADR-0004 D6**, **ADR-0005**, **PRD §10.1/§11**.
**Stops (3, this step) — all resolved.** SLURM **submit-ack** (§9.3): user acked → job 464 submitted. **Go/no-go
verdict** (§7 item 4): **GO** (a decisive pass, not borderline → no fallback stop-and-ask). **ADR-0002 amendment**
sign-off (§7 item 2): **A6 accepted as drafted** (records the GO verdict + finalizes the go/no-go thresholds).
**Commit.** Harness commit `1213883` + the measured result commit (report + `dvc add` checkpoint + ADR-0002 A6 +
this stanza) on `p1-07-seg-smoke-gonogo`. Feeds **P1-08** (seeded re-run reproducibility, exit-gate deliverable) +
**P2** (Stage-1 training — transfer confirmed).

## Step P1-08 — 2026-07-13: Verify Stage-1 smoke reproducibility (seeded re-run → deterministic within tolerance) — P1 exit-gate deliverable (MEASURED: REPRODUCIBLE — "smoke run reproducible" P1 exit condition met)
**Inputs.** The committed **P1-07 harness** `src/tbox_finder/train/stage1_smoke.py::main` (**unchanged** — reused as-is)
+ its committed config `conf/train/stage1_smoke.yaml` (seed 42, `PYTHONHASHSEED=0`, TF32/cudnn-off determinism §8.3); the
P1-06 held-out smoke set `data/interim/p1_seg_smoke/{windows.parquet,labels.npy}` (staged on the cluster checkout from
P1-07); the **P1-07 reference report** `reports/p1/seg_smoke_gonogo.json` (A6: min-core-F1 **0.99990**, verdict **GO**,
git-tracked). **Process.** A new **`slurm/p1/seg_smoke_repro.sbatch`** (SLURM `gpu` A4000, `tbox-ml-dna`) re-runs the
identical harness+config with **only** the two output paths overridden via Hydra (`report_path=reports/p1/seg_smoke_repro.json`,
`checkpoint_dir=checkpoints/p1/seg_smoke_repro`) so the re-run **cannot clobber** the committed P1-07 report or its DVC
checkpoint (override-composition verified by a local `hydra.compose` smoke: both paths land, seed/epochs/rc-combine
unchanged). The sbatch **mirrors the A6 hardening** (flock singleton on a distinct `.seg_smoke_repro.lock`, stale-artifact
clearing incl. checkpoint/provenance, DONE gate requiring report+checkpoint+provenance). A new pure-stdlib gate module
**`src/tbox_finder/train/repro.py`** (bare-importable, no numpy/torch) adjudicates the **two-level ADR-0002 A7 gate**:
**primary** = the go/no-go **verdict reproduces** (GO); **secondary** = **max abs diff ≤ τ = 1e-3 over the go/no-go-decision
metrics** {min_core_f1, the 3 core per_element_f1, macro/micro} (the 8 per-class F1s reported as a determinism diagnostic,
not τ-gated — see the scope decision below); it fails closed on a config/backbone mismatch, a non-finite metric, or any
drift from the 14-metric structural floor. **Outputs.** `slurm/p1/seg_smoke_repro.sbatch` +
`src/tbox_finder/train/{__init__ (adds repro),repro}.py` + `tests/ml/test_smoke_reproducible.py` (21) + **ADR-0002 A7**
(pinned a-priori + P1-08 scope re-sign-off) + the **measured** `reports/p1/seg_smoke_repro.json` (git-tracked; the repro
checkpoint is a throwaway DONE-gate signal, left on the cluster, not DVC) + this stanza. No result was fabricated (§10.3).
**Validation. MEASURED: REPRODUCIBLE** (SLURM job 466, cluster A4000 sm_86, seed 42, git_sha `d22a9fb`). **Primary:**
verdict **GO→GO**. **Secondary (τ=1e-3 over the decision metrics): PASS — gated max|Δ| = 2.90e-4** (`macro_f1` the max;
`min_core_f1` 0.999896→0.999820, Δ 7.6e-5; all 3 core `per_element_f1` Δ ≤ 1.8e-4). **Determinism diagnostic (reported,
not gated): max|Δ| = 1.67e-3 on `per_class_f1.Stem_III`** (Stem_II 5.7e-4; rest ≤ 1.8e-4) — the re-run is within-tolerance,
**not bit-exact** (the A2 C2 Mamba kernel), confirming the metric-level tolerance is the right instrument. This satisfies
the PRD §18.1 "smoke run reproducible" exit condition. **Harness PASS (21 tests):** every `repro.py` guard bites —
identical→reproducible (max|Δ|=0.0), sub-τ gated perturbation→reproducible, |Δ| exactly τ→inclusive-pass, gated
|Δ|>τ→**not** reproducible (even with GO==GO), **a diagnostic-only drift > τ → does NOT fail the gate** (the Stem_III case),
verdict-flip→**not** reproducible, config/backbone mismatch→fail-closed (incl. a backbone block **absent** or with an
**explicitly-null** revision on **both** reports), a re-run-only / symmetrically-dropped metric→fail-closed (the 14-metric
structural floor), non-finite metric→fail-closed, a **loosened/non-finite tolerance override→`ValueError`** (τ not
weakenable), and the `REPRO_TOLERANCE`/`EXPECTED_METRIC_KEYS`/gated-vs-diagnostic partition drift guards; **Tier 2
(committed-report, pure JSON) PASSES** on both committed reports under `TBOX_REQUIRE_REPRO_GATE=1`. Bare suite 546 passed
/ 124 skipped (no regression); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean. Verification: artifact-based (job 466, `sacct` off) —
`squeue` empty + `seg_smoke_repro.DONE` + a self-validated report; `.err` benign (wandb-offline notices only, 1041 B, no
Traceback/CUDA error).
**Decisions.** (1) **Harness unchanged** — reproducibility is a re-run of the *same* code, so P1-08 adds only a sbatch +
a comparison module + a test, never touches `stage1_smoke.py`. (2) **τ pinned a priori over all 14 metrics** (before the
re-run) so the gate is pre-registered, not fit to the observed diff (§8.3/§10.3). (3) **Scope decision (P1-08 re-sign-off,
§7 item 4):** the a-priori all-14 gate failed on **one rare non-core class (Stem_III, 1.67e-3 > τ)** while the verdict + all
go/no-go-decision metrics reproduced within τ (gated max 2.90e-4). Per the pre-registered §7 stop-and-ask (τ **not**
weakenable), the τ was **scoped to the decision metrics** (unchanged 1e-3, NOT loosened) and the per-class table reported
as a determinism diagnostic — justified because the go/no-go is *defined* on the 3 core elements + verdict (ADR-0004 D6),
and rarer non-core classes span fewer positions ⇒ larger per-argmax-flip F1 sensitivity (~1e-4→~1.7e-3) under the
irreducibly-nondeterministic Mamba kernel (A2 C2 / `use_deterministic_algorithms(warn_only=True)`). Rejected: widen τ to
2e-3 (looser claim the decision metrics don't need); hold-all-14-and-fail (misrepresents a successful decision-reproduction;
no nondeterministic op to chase). The rare-class drift is **disclosed, not hidden** (§10.3). context7 §3.3: n/a (unchanged
P1-07 harness; `repro.py` pure stdlib; Hydra override verified by an executed `hydra.compose` smoke under hydra 1.3.2).
Cross-link **ADR-0002 A7 / A6 / D7**, **ADR-0004 D6**, **ADR-0005**, **PRD §8.3/§11/§18.1**.
**Stops (3, this step) — all resolved.** **ADR-0002 A7 a-priori sign-off** (§7 item 2): τ = 1e-3 (recorded 2026-07-12).
**SLURM submit-ack** (§9.3): user acked → job 466 submitted. **A7 scope re-sign-off** (§7 item 4, validation-gate decision):
user chose to **scope τ to the decision metrics** after the all-14 gate tripped only on the diagnostic Stem_III (recorded
2026-07-13).
**Commit.** Setup commit `d22a9fb` (sbatch + `repro.py` + test + ADR-0002 A7 + stanza) + the measured result commit
(repro report + the scoped gate + CI `TBOX_REQUIRE_REPRO_GATE=1` + this stanza update) on `p1-08-smoke-repro`. **Closes the
PRD §18.1 P1 exit-gate item "smoke run reproducible."** No DVC artifact (report git-tracked; repro checkpoint throwaway).

## Step P1-09 — 2026-07-13: Segmentation-head fallback — frozen-embedding + per-position linear/CRF probe (non-gated baseline; PRD §18.2 exit-gate deliverable)
**Inputs.** Frozen Caduceus-PS **per-position** hidden states (P1-02, pinned revision `d89eeb85…`) of the P1-06 held-out
smoke set (`data/interim/p1_seg_smoke/{windows.parquet,labels.npy}` — 300 leave-clade-out loci, **93,764 valid positions**,
§9.2); the P1-03 `SegmentationHead` (Linear + optional in-repo linear-chain CRF) via the P1-04 `Stage1Segmenter(backbone=None)`
frozen-embedding entry.
**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/probes/frozen_seg_probe.py::run_seg_probe` — two-stage **single-env torch** (`tbox-ml-dna`):
`--stage extract` freezes the backbone (eval, `requires_grad_(False)`) and caches its per-position hidden states **once**
(GPU forward, batch=1, no pad); `--stage probe` trains **only the seg head** (backbone frozen; the `concat` identity
RC-combine is parameter-free) with per-nt **focal CE** (γ=2, PRD §11) on the cached embeddings, decodes (argmax; CRF Viterbi
when `use_crf`), and grades per-nt 8-class F1 via the same `metrics` unit as P1-07. Reuses `stage1_smoke`
(loader/tokenizer/determinism/focal/metrics) verbatim.
**Outputs.** `reports/p1/frozen_seg_probe.json` (git; **measured**) + gitignored regenerable
`data/interim/probes/frozen_seg_embeddings.npz` (192 MB, `*.npz`-ignored). **Every one of 300 windows decodes to a valid
length-L 8-class segmentation** (`all_windows_valid_8class=true`, min/max predicted class 0/7); per-nt **macro-F1 0.2647**,
micro-F1 0.4901; per-element **Stem_I 0.555 / Antiterminator 0.351 / Specifier 0.000** → **min-core-F1 0.0**; all 8 per-class
F1 reported. New `frozen_seg_probe.py` + `tests/unit/test_frozen_seg_probe.py` (**41 tests**: stdlib validator-bite tier
[incl. +extra-class, +per-element range, +invalid-decode-flag, +reuse drift guard from the pre-commit review] + CPU torch
probe tier [learns separable / can't fake noise on a held-out split / CRF-valid / only-head-trains] + GPU extract tier) +
`probes/__init__.py`.
**Validation.** **PASS** (non-gated deliverable): produces a valid per-position 8-class segmentation for every window **and** a
**reported** per-nt F1; the report self-validates fail-closed. **No threshold** (PRD §10.1/§18.2). §9.2 held-out-only + §10.3
`eval_split="fine_tune_set"` honesty flags enforced by `validate_report`. Full unit suite **557 passed / 57 skipped** bare (no
regression); 41/41 pass under `tbox-ml-dna` (GPU extract included, `TBOX_REQUIRE_CADUCEUS=1`); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean.
**Decisions.** (1) **PRD §10.1/§18.2-sourced, non-gated** — a measured baseline, **not** a gate; ADR-0002 D7's go/no-go
thresholds belong to the *end-to-end* P1-07 smoke, not to this frozen-embedding probe (`gate.gated=false`, `binding=false`).
The measured frozen baseline (macro-F1 0.265; **Specifier 0.0** — the 3-nt codon, 0.9% of positions, unresolved by a linear
head) is **materially weaker than the P1-07 end-to-end GO (min-core-F1 ≈ 1.0)** — the honest frozen-feature ceiling for a
linear/CRF head, quantifying **why end-to-end is the default** and this is a safety net; built now per §18.2, **not triggered**
(P1-07 = GO). (2) **Single-env torch** (not the provisional two-env/sklearn hedge): the pinned input is the torch P1-03 head
and sklearn cannot express the CRF variant, so both stages run under `tbox-ml-dna` — [[no-env-has-torch-and-sklearn]]
resolved. (3) **Overparametrization caveat (disclosed):** a 512-d head over few *same-set* positions can memorize even noise
(features > samples), so the honesty bite-test evals on a **held-out** window split; production is immune (93,764 positions ≫
512), so the eval-on-fine-tune-set number reflects genuine frozen-feature fit, not memorization. (4) **Doc-hygiene flag
(NOT resolved unilaterally, §7 item 2):** ADR-0002 **A6's prose** labels "GTDB continued-pretraining" as "P1-09", but imp.md
+ TODO.md (+ PRD §10.1) agree P1-09 = *this* seg-head fallback build and GTDB-CPT is **P1-11** — an ADR-A6 step-number
annotation drift needing a sign-off-gated hygiene amendment; flagged, not fixed (TODO.md's lookahead correction already
adjudicated imp.md-authoritative). (5) **Pre-commit adversarial 4-lens review workflow** (correctness / ADR-PRD faithfulness /
test-adequacy / reuse-integration → adversarial verify; 10 agents) → **5 confirmed findings, all fixed** before push: the
runtime backbone-revision guard was a **tautology** (`build_seg_report` hardcoded `revision=REVISION`; now **cache-sourced**
via `emb_meta.get("revision")` so a revision-less/foreign cache → `None` → validator fires, fail-closed) + 4 test-strengthening
(per-element range bite, extra-class bite, invalid-decode-flag test, reused-helper drift guard); 1 finding refuted. context7
§3.3: reuses P1-07-verified torch idioms (AdamW / focal `F.cross_entropy` reduction=none / `no_grad`) + the P1-05 npz-cache
pattern — no new third-party API surface. Cross-link **PRD §10.1/§18.2/§11**, **ADR-0002 D7/A6**, **ADR-0004 D1/D6**,
**ADR-0005**.
**Commit.** `<SHA>` on `p1-09-frozen-seg-probe` (feature branch → squash PR). **Builds the PRD §18.2 P1 exit-gate item
"Segmentation-head fallback path ready."** No DVC artifact (report git-tracked; the 192 MB embeddings `.npz` is a gitignored
regenerable intermediate — the P1-05 report-hashes-the-cache precedent).

## Step P1-10 — 2026-07-13: NT-multispecies backbone as a Stage-1 transfer-fallback drop-in (last ADR-0002 D7 rung; built, NOT triggered)
**Inputs.** The `InstaDeepAI/nucleotide-transformer-v2-250m-multi-species` checkpoint pinned to immutable revision
`c0f0359229f36ff6bc3a021247eefe0a9c344bd1` (ESM arch, `hidden_size` 768, 24 layers, 6-mer tokeniser, 2048-token/~12 kb
context, ~235 M params); the P1-03 `SegmentationHead(input_dim)` + P1-04 `Stage1Segmenter.logits_from_hidden` contracts; a
seeded 300-nt DNA fixture. **Process.** `src/tbox_finder/models/nt_backbone.py::load_nt_multispecies` loads NT as
`AutoModelForMaskedLM(trust_remote_code=True, output_hidden_states=True)` **under the ml-dna `transformers` 4.57.5 ceiling**
(ADR-0002 D2/A4), rejecting any non-pinned revision **before** the remote-code import (RCE guard mirroring
`caduceus_backbone`); `interface_parity` runs a **real CPU forward** (NT is stock ESM — no `selective_scan_cuda`, no GPU) →
`(1, 51, 768)` final-layer hidden state → feeds it through **both** the direct `SegmentationHead(768)` **and** the existing
`Stage1Segmenter(rc_combine="concat", d_model=384).logits_from_hidden` (concat = pure identity pass-through; NT is
single-strand so the RC interpretation does not apply — `d_model=384` only sizes the head to `2*384==768`) → both emit
`(1, 51, 8)` finite logits; `main` writes a **MEASURED** `provenance.json`. **Outputs.**
`src/tbox_finder/models/nt_backbone.py` (loader + `hidden_states`/`interface_parity`/`validate_report`/CLI; lazy heavy
imports → bare-importable) + `conf/model/nt_multispecies.yaml` (pinned spec; not selected by any train config) +
`tests/unit/test_nt_backbone.py` (**41 tests**: bare-tier validator-bite/pure/conf-drift/not-wired-as-default + gated
load-tier) + `data/interim/nt_multispecies_v2_250m/provenance.json` (git-tracked, no DVC — weights pulled from HF by
revision, not stored; 235,120,780 params; **300 nt → 51 tokens = 5.88× k-mer collapse** quantified).
**Validation.** Unit green — **41 passed** (bare 37 + the 3 load-tier under `TBOX_REQUIRE_NT=1`, HF cache offline + the
committed-provenance test); `gate.overall_pass=true` (`hidden_dim_ok` ∧ `seg_head_dropin_ok` ∧ `segmenter_dropin_ok`);
**not wired as default** (a test scans `conf/train/*.yaml`); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean; full bare unit suite 536
passed (no regression). **Decisions.** (1) **Checkpoint = v2-250m is an implementer choice** — ADR-0002 pins no NT
checkpoint (confirmed); v2 (RoPE, ~12 kb context) materially larger than the 7.73 M Caduceus-PS yet laptop/A4000-sized; a
one-line `REPO_ID`/`REVISION` swap to the 500 m sibling. (2) **§10.1 scientific-evidence gate CLEARED** with **≥2
independent agreeing authoritative sources** for the asserted claim *multispecies + prokaryote-inclusive via **bacteria***:
the peer-reviewed paper (**PMID:39609566; DOI:10.1038/s41592-024-02523-z**, Methods "Multispecies dataset") + the HF
pretraining-corpus dataset card `InstaDeepAI/multi_species_genomes` (Bacteria 667 species / 17.1 B nt of 850). **Archaea is
NOT asserted** (unresolved — no corpus-card row; a validator rule forbids `archaea_included=True`) and the per-checkpoint
**model cards are silent** on prokaryotes (recorded `model_card_states_prokaryotes=False`) — §10.3 no-overclaim; cleared
autonomously per §10.1 (agreement ⇒ proceed) + the P1-02 precedent. (3) **k-mer caveat documented** (load-bearing for P2):
the 6-mer tokeniser makes the seg head per-6-mer-token, so the ADR-0004 per-nt 8-class scheme does **not** align 1:1 with NT
tokens — a token→nt label broadcast is a P2-if-triggered concern, not built here. (4) **Pre-commit adversarial 5-lens review
workflow** (correctness / ADR-PRD faithfulness / test-adequacy / reuse-integration / scientific-honesty → adversarial
verify; 6 agents) → **1 confirmed finding (nit), fixed** before push (the conf↔code drift-guard omitted `max_tokens`, the one
echoed checkpoint scalar left unguarded → added the assert), 4 lenses clean, 0 refuted survivors. context7 §3.3:
transformers/huggingface_hub quota-blocked → the **installed-library cross-check substitutes** (the NT forward ran under the
pinned `transformers` 4.57.5, confirming the `AutoModelForMaskedLM(...trust_remote_code=True, output_hidden_states=True)` API
+ `hidden_states[-1]` extraction — the P1-02 precedent). Cross-link **PRD §10.1/§18.1**, **ADR-0002 D7/D2/A4/A6**,
**ADR-0004 D1** (the 8-class scheme the head consumes).
**Commit.** `<SHA>` on `p1-10-nt-backbone` (feature branch → squash PR). **Builds the PRD §18.1 P1 exit-gate item "Transfer
fallback ladder executable — NT-multispecies backbone drop-in built" (P1-10).** No DVC artifact (provenance git-tracked; NT
weights fetched from HF by pinned revision, not stored).

## Step P1-11 — 2026-07-13: Author the probe-triggered GTDB continued-pretraining (MLM) fallback harness
**Inputs.** Caduceus-PS backbone (P1-02, loaded with its native MLM head at the pinned revision); an *expected*
GTDB-prokaryotic DNA shard (`data/interim/gtdb_cpt/shard.fasta[.gz]` — does **not** exist in the repo, sourced at
trigger-time, asserted-not-fabricated §10.2/§10.3); `tbox_finder.train.stage1_smoke` thresholds (re-clear gate reuse).
**Process.** ADR-0002 D7 fallback #2 (full-FT default → **GTDB continued-pretraining** → NT-multispecies) is
**probe-triggered and — the P1-07 verdict being GO — NOT run**; the deliverable is the *authored, executable* harness
(PRD §18.1 fallback-triggered gate, operationalized in imp.md L902 as "authored + probe-triggered only"). Built
`src/tbox_finder/train/continued_pretrain.py::{run_cpt,main}` — a BERT-style masked-LM continued-pretraining loop
(15% mask / 80-10-10, *implementer choice*; PRD/ADR-0002/ADR-0003/imp pin no objective/rate) over the Caduceus
`AutoModelForMaskedLM`, computing its own **`-100`-ignored** MLM cross-entropy (the built-in loss ignores `[PAD]`=4 not
`-100`), a per-epoch-shuffled DDP-disjoint window schedule (`window_index_stream` reusing `_epoch_order`), a linear
warmup+decay LR schedule, `accelerate` multi-GPU DDP, and a fail-closed self-validating report; added
`load_caduceus_ps_for_masked_lm` to `caduceus_backbone.py` (reusing the P1-02 revision-guard-before-remote-import),
`conf/train/gtdb_cpt.yaml` (`@package _global_`; seed 42) + `conf/optim/adamw_cpt.yaml` (lr 1e-4 < the 3e-4 fine-tune),
and `slurm/p1/gtdb_continued_pretrain.sbatch` (gpu, gres a4000:4, `accelerate launch`, node-local `/tmp` build → move
final checkpoint to HOME, `PYTHONPATH=src`, EXIT-trap cleanup, DONE-marker gate). **Outputs.**
`src/tbox_finder/train/continued_pretrain.py` + `caduceus_backbone.py` (+loader) + `conf/train/gtdb_cpt.yaml` +
`conf/optim/adamw_cpt.yaml` + `slurm/p1/gtdb_continued_pretrain.sbatch` + `tests/unit/test_continued_pretrain.py`
(51 tests: bare masking/window/validator/conf-drift/not-auto-run tier + torch CPU MLM-CE/train-loop/scheduler/
write-outputs-roundtrip/RCE-guard tier). No measured artifact + **no ADR-0002 amendment** (the amendment records an
OUTCOME, moot when not run; the P1-10 authored-fallback precedent). **Validation.** Authoring gate PASSED — 51 tests
green (45 bare + 6 torch under `tbox-ml-dna` CPU), full bare unit suite **583 passed** (no regression), ruff 0.15.15 +
black 25.11.0 clean, Hydra `train/gtdb_cpt` composes + builds a `CPTConfig`, `accelerate launch` flags valid for
accelerate 1.12.0, sbatch `bash -n` clean. The **runtime** gate (CPT completes; re-run go/no-go min-core-F1 improves vs
P1-07 & clears ADR-0002 A6 ≥ 0.30) is **moot unless triggered**. **Decisions.** (1) **MLM objective + 15% masking rate +
budget are implementer choices** — PRD §10.1/ADR-0002 D7/ADR-0003 D7/imp.md pin none; the GPU-hour budget is a **RULE
with numeric frozen at P1 first measurement** (ADR-0003 D7), so `max_steps` + sbatch `--time` are recorded as
**provisional** bounds and **no GPU-hour figure is fabricated** (§10.3). (2) Reused the native `CaduceusForMaskedLM` head
(true *continued* pretraining) rather than a fresh Linear head; compute own `-100`-ignored MLM CE. (3) **Pre-commit
adversarial 6-lens review workflow** (correctness / ADR-PRD / test-adequacy / reuse / honesty / slurm-safety →
adversarial refute; 25 agents) → **17 confirmed / 19 raw**; fixed all real defects — a **major** missing `PYTHONPATH=src`
(accelerate workers would `ModuleNotFoundError`), a warmup dead-knob (→ real LR scheduler + honest `optim.lr_schedule`),
`_epoch_order` dead code (→ wired into the shuffled DDP schedule), sbatch `$BUILD` EXIT-trap + non-fatal cp + derived
`--num_processes`, and 8 test bite-gaps (label-is-original, overlapping-stride, schema_version, block-missing/`_prob01`/
`masked_token_count` mutators, `min_window_nt` pin, `_write_outputs` round-trip). (4) **⚠️ Flagged, NOT resolved (§7 item
2):** ADR-0002 **A6 mislabels this pass "P1-09"** (canonical = P1-11; imp.md L814); a sign-off-gated ADR hygiene
amendment (queued for the P1 exit gate, already noted in P1-09). context7 §3.3: `/huggingface/accelerate` (Accelerator
prepare/backward/clip) + `/huggingface/transformers` (MaskedLMOutput.logits `(B,L,vocab)`) consulted; cross-checked
against installed accelerate 1.12.0 / transformers 4.57.5. Cross-link **PRD §10.1/§15/§18.1**, **ADR-0002 D7/A6**,
**ADR-0003 D7**. **Commit.** `<SHA>` on `p1-11-gtdb-cpt` (feature branch → squash PR). No DVC artifact (harness authored;
the CPT checkpoint is produced only IF triggered).

## Step P1-12 — 2026-07-13: Prepare the ArchiveII nine-family LOFO benchmark + SS base-pair-F1 harness + sourced RiNALMo parity target
**Inputs.** The ArchiveII nine-family inter-family ("fam-fold") leave-one-family-out splits RiNALMo evaluates on
(RiNALMo's `remote_data.json` `ARCHIVEII_SPLITS` = Szikszai et al. 2022 `marcellszi/dl-rna` `ct-splits.tar.gz`,
`.ct` format); RiNALMo's published inter-family LOFO F1 (PMC12219582 / DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60872-5); the RiNALMo
SS-prediction protocol (sequence-only). **Process.** Built `src/tbox_finder/eval/archiveii_lofo.py` (pure-stdlib,
bare-CI-importable): fail-loud `.ct` parser (sequence + reference base pairs, symmetry/contiguity/length checks), the
`build_lofo` benchmark builder (canonical records = each family's `test/` set; per-fold train/valid/test counts), the
`base_pair_prf` **base-pair-F1 metric with ±1-nt slippage** matching RiNALMo `rinalmo/utils/sec_struct.py` (+ canonical-
pair / sharp-loop-min-dist-4 helpers), and `stage_archiveii` (checksummed download → verify pinned SHA-256 fail-loud →
extract → parse → write `provenance.json`). Reuses `ingest.record_hash`/`records_digest` (golden digest) +
`provenance.git_sha`/`sha256_file`. Added `workflow/rules/backbones.smk::archiveii_lofo_prep` (LOCAL one-time fetch, out
of `rule all`, dest derived from output → `--lint`-clean). **Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/eval/{__init__,archiveii_lofo}.py`;
committed `data/external/archiveii_lofo/provenance.json` (pinned tarball `sha256=0ff72090…`, **extraction-derived**
per-family/per-fold counts, `lofo_digest=69cae9aa…`; `.gitignore` un-ignores only this manifest, the ~1.3 MB `folds.json`
+ .ct files stay ignored per §5.2); `reports/p1/rinalmo_published_target.json` (the sourced parity target); golden
`tests/golden/test_archiveii_lofo.py` (+ `tests/fixtures/archiveii_lofo/{mini/…18 real .ct, expected.sha256=c97dd5d3…}`);
unit `tests/unit/test_ss_metric.py`. **Validation.** **PASSED** — golden: `build_lofo(fixture)` digest **and** an
independent stdlib re-parse both == `expected.sha256` (`c97dd5d3…`); SS-F1 hand-checks correct (slippage flips a 1-nt near
miss 0→1.0, no-slip stays 0; both-defined-zero → F1 0.0 not nan; empty pred/ref → nan); the committed provenance + target
JSON pass consistency + §10.3 sourced-not-fabricated checks. 24 P1-12 tests green; full bare suite **613 passed / 126
skipped, no regression**; ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 clean; `snakemake --lint` good + `-n` resolves. Full extraction
validated the production shape (`require_full`): **3865 records / 9 families**, per-family {5S 1283, SRP 918, tRNA 557,
tmRNA 462, RNaseP 454, grp1 74, 16S 67, telomerase 35, 23S 15}, each fold `test==family` & `train+valid==complement`.
**Decisions.** (1) **Scientific-evidence gate (§10.1) CLEARED autonomously** — the published inter-family LOFO F1 (Supp.
Table S2 **F1 non-weighted** column) reproduced **exactly** by an independent pymupdf SI-PDF extraction and by ≥3
corroborating reads (Table S2 + Table S3 INF pattern + main-text Fig. 4a), matching ADR-0002 D5 to 2 dp → agreement ⇒
proceed (P1-10 precedent). Recorded the published 3 dp values (mean 0.719; tRNA 0.931 highest, telomerase 0.120 lowest)
**and** the ADR-pinned 2 dp set + N=2 pp parity margin + telomerase carve-out; flagged the **non-weighted** column
explicitly (the length-weighted column differs and is NOT the target). (2) **No F1 SD is published** (Fig. 4b box/violin
only) → the PRD §10.2 "within published SD" criterion is unusable and the ADR-0002 D5 ±N pp fallback governs (`sd_reported:
false`). (3) **Per-family counts derived by extraction, not quoted** — the literature disagrees on the small families and
an adversarial recon pass mis-mapped {74,67,35,15}; the real mapping (grp1 74, 16S 67, telomerase 35, 23S 15) comes from
the data (§10.3, no fabrication). (4) **Pair-list golden hash, not dot-bracket** — ArchiveII contains pseudoknots (a 16S
example), so the digest hashes the lossless sorted pair set. (5) **Metric protocol adversarially corrected** from RiNALMo
code: ±1 slippage + canonical-only + sharp-loop-min-dist=4 confirmed; pseudoknots are removed on the *prediction* side
(a P1-13 model-runner concern), so the metric here scores whatever pair sets it is given. (6) **Pre-commit adversarial
5-lens review workflow** (correctness / ADR-PRD / test-adequacy / reuse / sci-honesty → 3-vote refute-by-default; 29
agents) → **8 raw / 6 survivors, all fixed before push**: a **major** RiNALMo-parity correction — an adversary fetched
`lbcb-sci/RiNALMo rinalmo/utils/sec_struct.py` and found it uses sklearn `zero_division=0.0`, so `base_pair_prf` now
scores an **empty prediction/reference 0.0 (not nan)** to mirror it (else a single empty-prediction record would poison
the P1-13 per-family mean vs the published 0.72); + a **major** test-coverage gap (the load-bearing `validate_manifest(
require_full=True)` nine-family/3865/per-fold arithmetic was CI-dark → 6 hand-built-manifest bite-tests); + a
provenance↔target digest/count cross-check (the two committed full-corpus constants CI can't recompute); + `parse_ct`
empty-file / malformed-line guards; + a `_safe_extract` tar-path-traversal bite-test; + the arXiv corroboration tagged
`(preprint, not peer-reviewed)` (§10.1). 2 refuted (a dormant `relax_pairs` `seq_len=None` branch; a `_sha256_file`
passthrough — inlined anyway). **⚠️ Flagged, NOT resolved (§7 item 2):** ADR-0002 D5's prose cites the
parity numbers to "Fig. 4a + box-plots 4b", but the exact numeric values live in **Supplementary Table S2 (non-weighted
column)** — a citation-precision enrichment (values are correct) queued as a sign-off-gated ADR hygiene amendment for the
P1 exit gate. context7 §3.3: biopython has no `.ct` reader (stdlib parser; numpy not needed — pure-Python sets). Cross-link
**PRD §10.2**, **ADR-0002 D5**. **Commit.** `<SHA>` on `p1-12-archiveii-lofo` (feature branch → squash PR). Feeds **P1-13**
(the RiNALMo mirror parity verification consumes these splits + metric + target).
